description: One-pass review of crate secretx-gcp-sm (secretx-gcp-sm/). Filed by /review-rusty-crate. Two passes: my-oss correctness + rust-idiom. Review-only, no code changes.
notes:
My-OSS Pass 1 complete. Maintainer risk: MEDIUM. Code is well-structured and defensively written (CRC32C, char validation, pinned-version put guard, zeroization), but has a P0 MSRV-broken issue (workspace-wide), and four P1 items: 401-contract conflict, no HTTP timeout (workspace-wide), no mock-server tests of HTTP error paths, and silently-optional CRC32C check. None are silent-data-loss bugs; all are operational/contract correctness items. Six Section-6 defenses recorded for future-PR resistance.
Correctness confidence: 12% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: behavior under exotic GCP responses (response shapes I cannot observe without running against the live API beyond the integration tests), bearer_auth's exact memory lifecycle inside reqwest (would need to read reqwest internals to be sure), edge cases in SecretUri::parse percent-decoding interacting with our character validator (e.g., does a doubly-percent-encoded value sneak through). Uncertainty: did not exhaustively read reqwest 0.13 source for header storage; did not exhaustively read secretx-core SecretUri::parse for all percent-decode edge cases. Would raise confidence with: mock-server tests covering all error paths, fuzz testing of from_uri.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — the crate uses idiomatic Rust throughout (capture syntax, ?, idiomatic Option/Result chaining, Zeroizing wrapper, async_trait). Only one real footgun (std::sync::Mutex held across .await in a test, P2). Type design is mostly sound; the missing Debug impl is the most visible gap. No unsafe outside of necessary env_var test scaffolding (correctly justified with SAFETY comments and ENV_LOCK).
Rust idiom confidence: 8% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: subtle Deref/coerce patterns I may have not noticed (e.g., bearer_auth's exact accepted Display bound interaction with Zeroizing<String>); whether SecretVersion::Pinned should be Box<str> for memory layout (skipped as bikeshedding). Uncertainty: did not exhaustively read async_trait macro expansion to verify no hidden idiom violations; did not read clippy --all-targets across all clippy groups (only ran -W clippy::pedantic which is the noisier set; no nightly-only lints).
title: Review: secretx-gcp-sm rusty pass
